item.title: Mother’s Day Buffet - 05/10/2026 11:00am EDT to 05/10/2026 2:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 2460 OH-231, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Brooke and her mom Michelle know a thing or two about making memories together - and this Mother's Day, they want to help you make some of your own. Join us for our Mother's Day Buffet on May 10th from 11am-2:30pm featuring a special menu, sweet treats, and good company. Reservations are on the hour: 11am, 12pm, 1pm and 2pm! Adults $19.95 | Seniors $17.95 | Kids (5-9) $12.95 | 4 & under FREE Spots are limited — call us to reserve yours! 419-448-7699￼ Tuesday - Thursday 4pm-9pm Friday & Saturday 4pm-10pm
item.dc:creator: Carmie's BBQ & Grill

item.title: Mother’s Day Buffet - 05/10/2026 11:00am EDT to 05/10/2026 2:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 2460 OH-231, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Brooke and her mom Michelle know a thing or two about making memories together - and this Mother's Day, they want to help you make some of your own. Join us for our Mother's Day Buffet on May 10th from 11am-2:30pm featuring a special menu, sweet treats, and good company. Reservations are on the hour: 11am, 12pm, 1pm and 2pm! Adults $19.95 | Seniors $17.95 | Kids (5-9) $12.95 | 4 & under FREE Spots are limited — call us to reserve yours! 419-448-7699￼ Tuesday - Thursday 4pm-9pm Friday & Saturday 4pm-10pm
item.dc:creator: Carmie's BBQ & Grill

item.title: Mother's Day Buffet | Ironwood Restaurant - 05/10/2026 11:00am EDT to 05/10/2026 2:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 4399 OH-231, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us at Ironwood for a Mother’s Day she’ll truly feel. This year, we’re creating something special, an afternoon centered around great food, beautiful views, and meaningful time together. Mother’s Day Buffet at Ironwood Sunday, May 10th 11:00 – 2:00 PM $34.99 plus tax and tip for adults $9.99 kids 4-12 Children 3 and under are free Whether you’re celebrating Mom, Grandma, or someone who’s always shown up for you, this is your moment to slow down and make it count. There will be breakfast items, lunch fare, assorted salads, and desserts. Mark your calendars now and plan to spend your afternoon at Ironwood. Because she deserves more than just a meal, she deserves an experience.
item.dc:creator: Ironwood Steakhouse

item.title: "Colored Pencil Mountain Trees" Monday, May 11, 2026 12pm-3pm #05112601 - 05/11/2026 12:00pm EDT to 05/11/2026 3:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA In this class you will use colored pencils to create a 5"x7" landscape drawing of mountain trees, learning about strokes and blending techniques. All supplies are provided. Preregistration is required by Friday, May 8. Minimum class size is 3 people. The cost is $45 for TAG members & $55 for non-members. Contact Mike Fink (813-695-0506), or TAG (419-443-0478), or tiffinartguild@att.net with questions. You can also stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Art Guild

item.title: Buckeye Lady Beetle Blitz Community Science Program Training - 05/11/2026 6:00pm EDT to 05/11/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Buckeye Lady Beetle Blitz Community Science Program Training 2026 Lady beetles are important insect predators that feed on landscape and agricultural pests. Unfortunately, some of our native Ohio lady beetles are in decline. YOU can help us track remaining populations! - You must have a home garden- food or flower - You will collect for 1 week in June and 1 week in August - We provide all the materials Interested? Register at go.osu.edu/blbbseneca
item.dc:creator: Ohio State University Extension - Seneca County

item.title: AEC RUMMAGE SALE - 05/12/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/16/2026 11:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 28 Hopewell Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Drop off May 12 & 13, 9:00 AM -3:30 PM. Come in and shop May 14 & 15, 8:30 AM - 3:30 PM & May 16, 8:30 AM-11:30 AM $1 BAG DAY
item.dc:creator: Allen Eiry Center

item.title: Downtown Tiffin Town Hall Meetings: May 2026 - 05/13/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/13/2026 10:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for the next session of the Downtown Tiffin Town Hall Series: Connecting People, Ideas, and Progress on Wednesday, May 13, 2026, hosted by the Design Committee at 9 AM. This session, titled “Design Matters: Creating a Downtown That Works and Welcomes,” will focus on design, beautification, and infrastructure, including parking. We’ll explore opportunities to improve downtown’s appearance and functionality through better signage, accessibility, and creative solutions for parking and walkability, all aimed at making Downtown Tiffin more inviting, accessible, and vibrant.
item.dc:creator: Downtown Tiffin

item.title: Red Cross Blood Drive - 05/13/2026 12:00pm EDT to 05/13/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 30 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA May 13, 2026 – Noon-6:00 PM Did you even wonder [...]
item.dc:creator: The Ritz Theatre

item.title: Downtown Tiffin Town Hall Meetings: May 2026 - 05/13/2026 7:00pm EDT to 05/13/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for the next session of the Downtown Tiffin Town Hall Series: Connecting People, Ideas, and Progress on Wednesday, May 13, 2026, at 7 PM, hosted by the Downtown Tiffin Design Committee. This session, titled “Design Matters: Creating a Downtown That Works and Welcomes,” will focus on design, beautification, and infrastructure, including parking. Attendees will have the opportunity to meet our Downtown Tiffin Design Committee volunteers and learn more about the work they are doing to enhance our community. We will also highlight ongoing initiatives such as Downtown Clean-Ups and the Best Foot Forward Golden Broom Award, which recognize efforts to keep downtown welcoming and well maintained. The discussion will be an open forum to explore ideas and gather input on improving downtown’s appearance and functionality through better signage, accessibility, and creative solutions for parking and walkability. Attendees are encouraged to share their perspectives, suggestions, and experiences as we work together to shape a more inviting, accessible, and vibrant Downtown Tiffin. Whether you are a business owner, resident, or community supporter, your voice will help guide future efforts and identify meaningful ways to get involved.
item.dc:creator: Downtown Tiffin

item.title: Downtown Trivia Night at the Chandelier - 05/14/2026 7:00pm EDT to 05/14/2026 8:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 151 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Groups of all ages are invited to compete in two rounds of general trivia questions plus the final questions of local interest. Answers will be judged by local celebrities. Participants are asked to form teams of 6 or fewer, which can be composed of all ages. Teams will write their answers to be judged by local celebrities. Questions will be geared toward ages 12 and up. No phones, tablets, or electronic devices may be accessed during the contest. A traveling trophy will be awarded to the winning team. **ONE GrowthZone ticket per team. List each of the team members on the ticket.
item.dc:creator: Downtown Tiffin

item.title: Downtown Trivia Night at the Chandelier - 05/14/2026 7:00pm EDT to 05/14/2026 8:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 151 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Groups of all ages are invited to compete in two rounds of general trivia questions plus the final questions of local interest. Answers will be judged by local celebrities. Participants are asked to form teams of 6 or fewer, which can be composed of all ages. Teams will write their answers to be judged by local celebrities. Questions will be geared toward ages 12 and up. No phones, tablets, or electronic devices may be accessed during the contest. A traveling trophy will be awarded to the winning team. **ONE GrowthZone ticket per team. List each of the team members on the ticket.
item.dc:creator: Carmie's BBQ & Grill

item.title: LIFEGUARDING COURSE - 05/15/2026 12:00am EDT to 05/17/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 180 Summit St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Ready to make a difference this summer? 🌊☀️

Become a certified lifeguard and gain the skills to save lives, build confidence, and become a leader!

Important Details:

MAY 15-17, 2026

Friday 4p-8p, Saturday 8a-5p, & Sunday 8a-5p

• Blended course format – 7 hours of online coursework must be completed before the first day of class

• Swim assessment required one week prior to the first class- Friday at 6pm

• Attendance is mandatory for all scheduled days (no make-up sessions)

• Registration closes one week before the class begins

• Limited to just 8 students per class – reserve your spot early!

Don’t wait—spots fill fast! 📅 Enroll now at the Tiffin Community YMCA and dive into a new opportunity!
item.dc:creator: YMCA of Bucyrus-Tiffin, Inc

item.title: Scrub Sale - 05/15/2026 9:30am EDT to 05/15/2026 11:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 182 St Francis Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Stop by St. Francis Senior Ministries for the scrub sale!
item.dc:creator: St. Francis Senior Ministries

item.title: Flower Plant Stand(s) Workshop - 05/15/2026 5:30pm EDT to 05/15/2026 8:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 113 Melmore St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Get her a gift certificate for Mother's Day! Grab your momma, grab some friends and come hang out at the studio; 5:30PM. Choose from Small, Medium or Large. Or do a combo pack!
item.dc:creator: Rustic Flair & Brush

item.title: Opening Day for Farmers Market - 05/16/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/16/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 195 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Capture the spirit of renewal at the first market of the season, where homemade goods and fresh produce come together to create a celebration of local talent
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Seneca Farmers Market

item.title: Gathered Mom’s Market! - 05/16/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/16/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 1925 W County Rd 26, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA There is something really special about women coming together with a shared purpose. We’re so excited to introduce the Gathered Moms Market, a space where local, mom-owned businesses can come together, be celebrated, and be supported by our community. This market will be held alongside a donation-based garage sale fundraiser as part of a local family’s adoption journey, making every purchase and every visit part of something so much bigger. You’ll find everything from handmade goods to baked treats, thoughtful gifts, and unique finds… all created by talented local moms. Mark your calendar and come spend the day with us! May 16th starting at 9 AM Fitch Homestead Farmstand Vendor spots are limited! Message me for details!
item.dc:creator: Fitch Homestead Farmstand

item.title: 2026 Tiffin Flea Market - 05/16/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/16/2026 4:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 100 Hopewell Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Come join us for a fun weekend of shopping for your next treasure.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Flea Market

item.description: Location : 144 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Enjoy from scratch made breakfast and lunch items featuring buzzworthy sourdough artisan breads

item.description: Location : 30 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Saturday, May 16, 2026 – 7:30 pm From the Toledo [...]

item.title: 2026 Tiffin Flea Market - 05/17/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/17/2026 3:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 100 Hopewell Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Come join us for a fun weekend of shopping for your next treasure.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Flea Market

item.title: Calvert High School Glass Students Present “Pieces of Us” Glass Art Show and Sale - 05/17/2026 12:00pm EDT to 05/17/2026 3:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 227 E Perry St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Calvert High School Glass Students Present “Pieces of Us” Glass Art Show and Sale Calvert High School’s glass art students Sydney Michaels, Zane Thallman, Cash Lee, Morgan Peiffer, Wesley Hickman, Lukas Stultz invite the community to attend “Pieces of Us,” a unique glass art show and sale showcasing student creativity and craftsmanship. The event will take place on **Sunday, May 17, from 12:00 p.m. to 3:00 p.m. **at The Poignon Project 227 E, Perry St. Tiffin, OH and is open to the public. Guests can enjoy light snacks and drinks while exploring a variety of handcrafted glass pieces created by students. A highlight of the afternoon will be a live flame-working competition from 12:30 p.m. to 1:30 p.m., where students will compete in multiple categories in a judged glassblowing-style event, demonstrating their skills and artistry in real time. All student artwork will be available for purchase, with 50% of proceeds going to each student and 50% of proceeds benefiting Calvert Catholic Schools. Funds raised will support the 2026–2027 glassblowing class and help offset the cost of a planned educational field trip. Please note that sales are cash only. This event offers a meaningful opportunity to support student artists while experiencing the beauty and intricacy of glass art up close. Event Details:�Title: Pieces of Us�Date: Sunday, May 17�Time: 12:00 p.m. – 3:00 p.m.�Location: The Poignon Project 227 E, Perry St. Tiffin, OH�Admission: Free and open to the public For more information, please contact The Poignon Project 419-618-6429
item.dc:creator: The Poignon Project

item.title: Calvert High School Glass Students Present “Pieces of Us” Glass Art Show and Sale - 05/17/2026 12:00pm EDT to 05/17/2026 3:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 227 E Perry St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Calvert High School’s glass art students Sydney Michaels, Zane Thallman, Cash Lee, Morgan Peiffer, Wesley Hickman, Lukas Stultz invite the community to attend “Pieces of Us,” a unique glass art show and sale showcasing student creativity and craftsmanship. The event will take place on **Sunday, May 17, from 12:00 p.m. to 3:00 p.m. **at The Poignon Project 227 E, Perry St. Tiffin, OH and is open to the public. Guests can enjoy light snacks and drinks while exploring a variety of handcrafted glass pieces created by students. A highlight of the afternoon will be a live flame-working competition from 12:30 p.m. to 1:30 p.m., where students will compete in multiple categories in a judged glassblowing-style event, demonstrating their skills and artistry in real time. All student artwork will be available for purchase, with 50% of proceeds going to each student and 50% of proceeds benefiting Calvert Catholic Schools. Funds raised will support the 2026–2027 glassblowing class and help offset the cost of a planned educational field trip. Please note that sales are cash only. This event offers a meaningful opportunity to support student artists while experiencing the beauty and intricacy of glass art up close. Event Details: Pieces of Us Date: Sunday, May 17, 2026 Time: 12:00 p.m. – 3:00 p.m. Location: The Poignon Project 227 E Perry St., Tiffin, OH� Admission: Free and open to the public For more information, please contact The Poignon Project 419-618-6429
item.dc:creator: Calvert Catholic Schools

item.title: Mothers Day High Tea & Brunch - 05/17/2026 1:00pm EDT to 05/17/2026 2:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 89 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Celebrate Mother’s Day with an exquisite High Tea Brunch on May 17th from 1 PM to 3 PM. Enjoy a delightful experience filled with delicious treats and warm moments perfect for honoring the special moms in your life.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Community Kitchen

item.title: Let's Talk About It - 05/18/2026 10:30am EDT to 05/18/2026 11:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Tiffin Seneca Public Library for "Let's Talk About It," an adult book discussion group on Monday, May 18 at 10:30 a.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. The featured book for discussion is "Three Days in June" by Anne Tyler, with copies available at the Library. Everyone is welcome to attend this free event, and no registration is required. Refreshments will be provided. For more information, please call the Library at 419-447-3751 or visit their online calendar at www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Plant Swap - 05/18/2026 12:00pm EDT to 05/18/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The Tiffin-Seneca Public Library will host a “Plant Swap” on Monday, May 18 from 12:00 to 6:00 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. Drop-in anytime between 12:00 and 6:00 p.m. to leave extra plants that are looking for a new home, or to take something new home. Please bring plants in soil or water. The Library will provide extra pots and soil. Suggestions of plants to bring are house plants, outdoor plants, starts, bulbs, and seeds. Please bring as much detail on the plant as possible, such as instructions for planting, or whether it requires sun or shade. Registration is not required for this free adult program. Plants can be dropped off at the Information Desk beginning at 9:30 a.m. For more information, call the Library at 419-447-3751 or visit www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Wings & Reconnection Scouting Alumni Event - 05/18/2026 6:00pm EDT to 05/18/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 100 S Shaffer Park Dr, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for the Wings & Reconnection Scouting Alumni Event, where you can enjoy great wings, share memorable stories, and support a great cause. This is an opportunity to reconnect with fellow Eagle Scouts, past Scouters, parents, leaders, and community supporters who cherish the values of Scouting. The event will take place at Buffalo Wild Wings in Tiffin, Ohio, on [insert date] from 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM. We are excited to welcome Guest Speaker Eagle Scout Samuel Faber from 6:00 PM to 6:30 PM. Thanks to the generosity of Tiffin Buffalo Wild Wings and Cris Lewis-Thrivent, this event is free for all attendees. Drop in anytime!
item.dc:creator: Arrowwood District, Scouting America

item.title: Project WET FREE Educator Workshop - 05/20/2026 10:00am EDT to 05/20/2026 3:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 194 St Francis Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA FREE workshop for formal and informal educators Project Wet (Water Education for Today) is hands-on and multiple interdisciplinary curriculum guides with over 60 water quality and quantity activities for grades K-12. The original guide was produced in 1995 and has changed many times over the years. Educators at this workshop will receive Water in Earth Systems and Foundations of Water Education Guides plus other resources produced by Project WET. Funding for the workshop guides is provided by ee360+ funding through NAAEE. Come learn, have fun, and make connections with other water educators. To register email dennis.clement@epa.ohio.gov. For additional information email Dennis or call 614.644.2048.
item.dc:creator: Ohio Environmental Protection Agency

item.title: AJ Heavenly Pizza Fundraiser - 05/20/2026 4:00pm EDT to 05/20/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 28 Hopewell Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for the AJ Heavenly Pizza Fundraiser at AJ's Pizza! Enjoy delicious food while supporting a great cause. Thank you for your participation!
item.dc:creator: Allen Eiry Center

item.title: MST Food Truck - 05/21/2026 10:00am EDT to 05/21/2026 2:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 182 St Francis Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA MST Food Truck will be in the front parking lot of St. Francis Home on Thursday, May 21st from 10am-2pm. Come out and get some good eats!
item.dc:creator: St. Francis Senior Ministries

item.title: MST Food Truck - 05/21/2026 10:00am EDT to 05/21/2026 2:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 182 St Francis Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA MST Food Truck will be in the front parking lot of St. Francis Home on Thursday, May 21st from 10am-2pm. Come out and get some good eats!
item.dc:creator: St. Francis Senior Ministries

item.title: Barking Lot Party - Third Thursday in Downtown Tiffin - 05/21/2026 5:00pm EDT to 05/21/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for a paw-some evening in Downtown Tiffin on Thursday, May 21, 2026, from 5 to 8 PM! Enjoy a pet-friendly night out with your furry friends as you stroll through downtown and discover a pet-friendly experience throughout the downtown district. Stay tuned for more details and a list of participating businesses! If your dog is aggressive toward other dogs, children, or strangers, we kindly ask that you leave them at home. All dogs must be leashed, and owners are responsible for bringing doggie bags and cleaning up after their pets. Mark your calendars and plan to join us for a fun and pet-friendly evening in Downtown Tiffin!
item.dc:creator: Downtown Tiffin

item.title: Farmers Market - 05/23/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/23/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 195 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Discover the joy of fresh produce, delightful baked goods, and stunning flowers, all waiting for you every Saturday morning!
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Seneca Farmers Market

item.title: Outdoor Adventure Day - 05/23/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/23/2026 10:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 3570 N County Rd 33, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join Tiffin Scout Troop 499 for Outdoor Adventure Day at the Izaak Walton League of America! There will be demonstrations by Tiffin Scout Troop 499 and ODNR as well as Fishing, Shooting, Archery & Food. All ages are welcome. This is a drop in event, no registration required.
item.dc:creator: Arrowwood District, Scouting America

item.title: Farmers Market - 05/23/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/23/2026 1:00pm EDT

item.title: YLFCU Memorial Day Parade 2026 - 05/25/2026 10:00am EDT to 05/25/2026 11:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA null
item.dc:creator: Your Legacy Federal Credit Union

item.title: The Tragic End - presented by Mike Gilbert - 05/26/2026 10:30am EDT to 05/26/2026 11:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 28 Hopewell Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The Tragic End: The Assassination of Robert F Kennedy - Mike Gilbert is a very interesting speaker. Please call Allen Eiry Center at (419) 448-9774 to save a seat.
item.dc:creator: Allen Eiry Center

item.title: Trivia Tuesday: Music - 05/26/2026 6:00pm EDT to 05/26/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The Tiffin-Seneca Public Library will host “Trivia Tuesday,” an adult trivia night on Tuesday, May 26 from 6:00 to 8:00 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. Join us for an evening of fun, facts, and friendly competition. Teams will battle it out over six exciting rounds designed to test your knowledge about music. Prizes for the winning team include a custom 3D-printed trophy made right here at the Library. This free program is open to both adults and teens. Space is limited so registration is required by visiting the online calendar at www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org. For more information, please call (419) 447-3751 or log onto www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Collaborative Luncheon | May - 05/27/2026 11:30am EDT to 05/27/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 248 Miami St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Welcome to the Seneca County Collaborative Luncheon series, where you have a seat at the table for a gathering filled with networking, education, promotion, and impact. On the menu… Date & Time: Wednesday, May 27, 2026 11:30 AM: Doors Open 11:30 AM - 12 PM: Networking 12 PM - 1 PM: Program Location: Tiffin University’s Marion Center Featured Speaker: Elected Officials Topic: Legislative Lunch Registration: $25 for members and non-members Our luncheon series is designed to provide a comprehensive experience that embodies the mission of the Collaborative and allows our organization to best serve you. Reserve your seat today at: https://senecaregionalchamberofcommerceoh.growthzoneapp.c... Experience the Collaborative one bite at a time. See you at the luncheon!
item.dc:creator: Seneca Regional Chamber & Development

item.title: How to Read a Book Like a Professor: Follow-Up Discussion - 05/28/2026 6:00pm EDT to 05/28/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for the “How to Read a Book Like a Professor: Follow-Up Discussion” on Thursday, May 28 at 6:00 p.m. in the Junior Home Room at the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library. This event, part of the 2026 Christopher D. English Community Read, features Heidelberg Professor Dr. Barry Devine returning to lead a discussion on "The Astral Library" by Kate Quinn. Participants will delve into the novel while reflecting on Dr. Devine’s earlier session and the reading techniques he shared. This initiative encourages the Seneca County community to engage with the selected title through various events and discussions. Additionally, on April 28 at 6:30 p.m., New York Times best-selling author Kate Quinn will speak at the Chandelier Community Event Center, followed by a book signing and the opportunity to purchase copies of "The Astral Library" through the Pan Yan Bookstore. For more details about the Community Read, visit www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/community-read.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Seneca Proud Book Club - 05/28/2026 6:30pm EDT to 05/28/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 137 E Perry St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Book club is open to everyone, even if you haven't finished reading the book yet. Books are available at Pan Yan Bookstore in downtown Tiffin and also through the public library.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Proud

item.title: "Birch Trees and Path" Thursday, May 28, 2026 9am-12pm or 6pm-9pm #05282601 or #05282602 - 05/28/2026 9:00pm EDT to 05/29/2026 12:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Pre-registration and payment is required by Friday, May 22. Paint "Birch Trees and Path" in water mixable oils with Carol Hoffman on Thursday, May 28 from 9am until 12 pm or from 6pm-9pm. Please arrive at least 15 minutes before class time. Carol will guide you through the process of creating this painting whether you are a beginner or more advanced painter. All of the materials including the 11"x14" canvas are included in the price of the class. Minimum class size 3 people, maximum of 8. Please bring a large box to take your painting home in. Cost is $50 for TAG members & $60 for non-members. Call Carol with questions. 419 624 7020, Tiffin Art Guild at 419-443-0478 (tiffinartguild@att.net), or stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Art Guild

item.title: LIFEGUARDING COURSE - 05/29/2026 12:00am EDT to 05/31/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 180 Summit St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Ready to make a difference this summer? 🌊☀️

Become a certified lifeguard and gain the skills to save lives, build confidence, and become a leader!

Important Details:

MAY 29-31, 2026

Friday 4p-8p, Saturday 8a-5p, & Sunday 8a-5p

• Blended course format – 7 hours of online coursework must be completed before the first day of class

• Swim assessment required one week prior to the first class- Friday at 6pm

• Attendance is mandatory for all scheduled days (no make-up sessions)

• Registration closes one week before the class begins

• Limited to just 8 students per class – reserve your spot early!

Don’t wait—spots fill fast! 📅 Enroll now at the Tiffin Community YMCA and dive into a new opportunity!
item.dc:creator: YMCA of Bucyrus-Tiffin, Inc

item.title: 5p | Summer Reading Kick-Off Party - 05/29/2026 5:00pm EDT to 05/29/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA null
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Farmers Market - 05/30/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/30/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 195 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Discover the joy of fresh produce, delightful baked goods, and stunning flowers, all waiting for you every Saturday morning!
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Seneca Farmers Market

item.title: Farmers Market - 05/30/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/30/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 195 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Discover the joy of fresh produce, delightful baked goods, and stunning flowers, all waiting for you every Saturday morning!

item.title: Breakfast at JAM’s Markets SATURDAYS 9-2pm - 05/30/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/30/2026 2:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 144 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Enjoy from scratch made breakfast and lunch items featuring buzzworthy sourdough artisan breads
item.dc:creator: JAM's Monthly Markets

item.title: Celebrate 30 Years—Park Scrabble - 05/30/2026 10:00am EDT to 05/30/2026 11:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 155 Miami St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA To help celebrate 30 years, the Seneca County Park District will host a “park scrabble” game  Hike the trail looking for letters to then place on the giant scrabble board.  How many “park” words can you create? Registration is required at least 24 hours in advance of the program.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Parks

item.title: Autumnwood Care Center 2nd Annual Motorcycle and Car show - 05/30/2026 11:00am EDT to 05/30/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 670 OH-18, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Autumnwood Care Center’s 2nd Annual Motorcycle & Car Show Join us for a great afternoon of vehicles, food, and community while supporting a meaningful cause! Saturday, May 30 11:00 AM – 1:00 PM Autumnwood Care Center 607 E State Route 18 Tiffin, Ohio 44883 This event will benefit the Seneca County Honor Bus, helping local veterans travel to Washington, D.C. to visit the memorials built in their honor. Event Highlights: • Motorcycle & Car Show • Cookout • 50/50 Raffle • Gift Basket Raffle All activities are by donation and the event is open to the public. Bring your friends, family, and your favorite ride and help us support our veterans! Questions? Call 419-447-7151
item.dc:creator: Autumnwood Care Center

item.title: Tiffin Butterflies - 05/30/2026 1:30pm EDT to 05/30/2026 2:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 194 St Francis Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for an introduction to 12-15 colorful butterfly species that are common locally during the summer, followed by a short walk looking for a few of them. Program led by Leonard Weber.
item.dc:creator: Franciscan Earth Literacy Center
